candidates.title: Encoder-Driven Feature Absorption: Mechanism and Causal Consequences
candidates.summary: New pilot evidence overturns prior narrative: absorption is driven by the encoder's learned alignment with hierarchical structure, not decoder geometry. H_Mech factorial shows trained encoder + random decoder achieves 0.49 absorption (matching full training at 0.484), while random encoder + trained decoder drops to 0.299. H3 steering fix shows absorbed features are 1.62x more sensitive to steering.
hypotheses: H1: Trained SAEs show higher multi-child proportional absorption than random baselines (PASSED)
H_Mech: Absorption is driven by encoder alignment, not decoder geometry (PASSED - new pilot)
H3: Steering absorbed features improves sensitivity (PASSED - 1.62x ratio, new pilot)
H2: Absorption correlates with feature frequency (FAILED - wrong direction)
candidates.pilot_focus: H_Mech 2x2 factorial (5 conditions), multi-seed validation (3 seeds), H3 steering fix. All completed in new pilots.

merge_contributions.pragmatist: Multi-child proportional ablation fixes pilot measurement failure.
merge_contributions.theoretical: Encoder-driven mechanism reframes covariance structure theory.
merge_contributions.interdisciplinary: Efficient coding (Barlow) replaces competitive exclusion as framing.
merge_contributions.contrarian: Geometric invariant baseline confirmed (Condition A = 0.299), but learned component is encoder-driven.
merge_contributions.empiricist: H3 steering fix provides causal validation; H_Mech factorial provides mechanism decomposition.

candidates.drop_trigger: If multi-seed validation shows absorption is purely random (no structure)

candidates.title: Safety-Critical Features Are Disproportionately Absorbed
candidates.summary: SAEs systematically fail to represent rare, safety-critical features due to absorption. SAE-based safety analysis is unreliable precisely for the cases that matter most. No prior work examines absorption in safety-critical features. Synthetic pilot failed (p=0.665) because synthetic features lack semantics -- real Gemma Scope SAEs required.
hypotheses: H_Safe: Safety-critical features have higher absorption rates than matched non-safety features (Mann-Whitney p < 0.05)
candidates.pilot_focus: Synthetic pilot failed (p=0.665) -- meaningless on random feature indices. Requires real Gemma Scope SAEs with Neuronpedia annotations.
candidates.novelty_note: No prior work examines whether safety-critical features are systematically more absorbed. Genuinely novel (9/10). Highest novelty per minute invested.
candidates.drop_trigger: If safety-relevant features do not show elevated absorption rates on real SAEs (p > 0.3)

candidates.title: Encoder-Driven Decomposition of Absorption
candidates.summary: 2x2 factorial design to decompose absorption into encoder alignment and decoder geometry components. New pilot evidence shows absorption is entirely encoder-driven. This is the core mechanistic contribution.
hypotheses: H_Mech: 2x2 factorial (random/trained encoder x random/trained decoder) reveals encoder as sole driver
candidates.pilot_focus: Completed: 4 conditions, 1 seed. Full validation: 5 seeds, 3 L0 targets, stochastic hierarchy.
candidates.novelty_note: First decomposition of absorption into encoder vs decoder contributions using factorial design.
candidates.drop_trigger: If all conditions show identical absorption

candidates.title: Steering Intervention Validation (H3 - FIXED)
candidates.summary: Fixed broken steering implementation. New pilot shows absorbed features are 1.62x more sensitive to steering than non-absorbed. Unexpected positive result -- absorbed features retain parent direction information.
hypotheses: H3: Steering absorbed features toward parent directions improves sensitivity (sensitivity ratio > 1.0)
candidates.pilot_focus: Completed: Fixed steering implementation, verified activation changes, measured sensitivity ratio = 1.62x.
candidates.novelty_note: Causal validation showing absorbed features are MORE sensitive to steering (1.62x ratio).
candidates.drop_trigger: If steering sensitivity ratio < 1.0 on stochastic data

candidates.title: Encoder Regularization to Reduce Absorption
candidates.summary: If H_Mech confirms encoder-driven absorption, modifying the encoder architecture or training objective could reduce absorption. This is a constructive intervention target opened by the H_Mech finding.
hypotheses: H_EncReg: Encoder regularization penalizing parent-child activation correlation reduces absorption >30% with <5% reconstruction degradation
candidates.pilot_focus: Not yet tested. Would require modifying SAE training loss.
candidates.novelty_note: First encoder-targeted intervention for absorption reduction (prior work modifies both encoder and decoder).
candidates.drop_trigger: If regularization degrades reconstruction >10% or reduces absorption <10%

candidates.title: Niche Geometry Diagnostic for Training-Free Absorption Prediction
candidates.summary: Training-free proxy for absorption prediction using convex geometry of decoder directions. DEPRIORITIZED because H_Mech shows decoder geometry is irrelevant to absorption.
candidates.pilot_focus: DEPRIORITIZED -- H_Mech shows decoder geometry contributes nothing.
candidates.novelty_note: Would need to operate on encoder directions instead of decoder directions.

candidates.title: Multi-Resolution SAE Ensemble for Hierarchical Feature Recovery
candidates.summary: Train ensemble of SAEs with varying L0 targets (16, 64, 256) to collectively recover hierarchical features. High-sparsity SAEs capture coarse parent features; low-sparsity SAEs capture fine child features.
hypotheses: H_Ens: High-L0 SAE captures child features that low-L0 SAE has absorbed
candidates.pilot_focus: Not yet tested. High cost (2 hours).
candidates.novelty_note: Overlaps with Gadgil et al. on ensemble concept but differentiated by L0 diversity mechanism.

pool_status.dropped_notes: cand_asym (encoder-decoder norm ratio asymmetry) dropped -- pilot data shows no separation (0.487 vs 0.471). cand_eco (competitive exclusion) dropped -- H2 failure undermines theoretical foundation, and H_Mech result suggests efficient coding not competitive exclusion.
